summary: Read-only R5 retry reviewed RAG/search quality completion language, verify:goal RAG completion classifier, RAG proof/eval implementation, hybrid retrieval mode behavior, MEMORY.md, and current data-vault RAG artifacts. Verdict: AGREE because the plan/docs and verify:goal now explicitly separate scaffold canary health from completion-grade semantic RAG/answer-quality evidence and verify:goal rejects local_text_hashing_v1 with semantic_embedding_provider=false as final completion.
decisions: No blocking objections found for the specific RAG/search quality completion-gate question.

Current artifacts still show scaffold canary health with local_text_hashing_v1 and semantic_embedding_provider=false, but verify:goal classifies that as rag_semantic_provider_not_configured or rag_text_hashing_scaffold_only / canary-only and therefore not completion-grade.
next_steps: Keep future work focused on implementing a real semantic embedding provider or documented local multilingual semantic model plus generated-answer quality metrics before final RAG completion can pass.
Optionally improve artifact wording so rag_proof/rag_eval visible_status says scaffold/canary healthy, but this is not required for the plan/completion-gate verdict.
